conflict: Competing desires to protect the eel, save the camp, save a marriage, and escape blame
consequence: The three-way relationship and the eel project are stripped of romance and revealed as entangled with use, class, and extraction.
must_land_beats: Mina shares the annex evidence and destroys the easy redemption narrative.
Imogen argues for publicity, Tomas argues from lost wages and humiliation, and Rhea refuses everyone's preferred simplification.
After the fight, no one can keep pretending sex has been separate from access.
